3349	[Dimension-shift induction \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\)]
3350	  \label{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}
3351	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis}
3352	  \uses{def:absolute_cohomology, lem:quotient_vanishing_cech,
3353	    lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib,
3354	    lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}
3355	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3356	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof, induction step) (read from
3357	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1695--1776).
3358	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Since $\mathcal{F}$ was an arbitrary $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
3359	  % with vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for all $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$
3360	  % we conclude that also $H^1(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0$ since $\mathcal{Q}$ is
3361	  % another of these sheaves (see above). By the long exact sequence this in
3362	  % turn implies that $H^2(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$. And so on and so forth."
3363	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3364	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3365	  Let \(X\), \(\mathcal{B}\), \(\mathrm{Cov}\) be as in
3366	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}. For every \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
3367	  \(\mathcal{F}\) with vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\), every
3368	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\), and every \(p > 0\), the absolute cohomology vanishes,
3369	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\).
3370	\end{lemma}
3371	
3372	\begin{proof}
3373	  \uses{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech, lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing,
3374	    lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib, lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}
3375	  Induct on \(p \ge 1\) with the statement quantified over \emph{all}
3376	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\) having vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology
3377	  for \(\mathrm{Cov}\) simultaneously, so that the quotient may take the role of
3378	  \(\mathcal{F}\) at the next stage. Fix such an \(\mathcal{F}\), embed
3379	  \(\mathcal{F} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{I}\) into an injective
3380	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and put \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\). By
3381	  Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech} the quotient \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has
3382	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\), so it is itself a valid
3383	  instance of the inductive statement.
3384	
3385	  \emph{Base case \(p = 1\):} immediate from
3386	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing} applied to \(\mathcal{F}\).
3387	
3388	  \emph{Step \(p \Rightarrow p+1\):} the covariant Ext long exact sequence
3389	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) of
3390	  \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) at \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\)
3391	  gives the exact strand
3392	  \[
3393	    H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) \xrightarrow{\ \delta\ } H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) \to
3394	      H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{I}).
3395	  \]
3396	  The right term vanishes since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective
3397	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}), and the left term
3398	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) by the inductive hypothesis applied to \(\mathcal{Q}\)
3399	  (admissible by the previous paragraph). Exactness forces
3400	  \(H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This completes the induction, giving
3401	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and all \(U \in \mathcal{B}\).
3402	\end{proof}

3404	\begin{lemma}
3405	[{\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis]

3413	  % basis-comparison criterion for {\v C}ech vs.\ sheaf cohomology), statement and

3415	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(Variant of Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-vanish}.)
3416	  % Let $X$ be a ringed space.
3417	  % Let $\mathcal{B}$ be a basis for the topology on $X$.
3418	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3419	  % Assume there exists a set of open coverings $\text{Cov}$
3420	  % with the following properties:
3421	  % \begin{enumerate}
3422	  % \item For every $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$
3423	  % with $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ we have
3424	  % $U, U_i \in \mathcal{B}$ and every $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}$.

3426	  % occurring in $\text{Cov}$ is a cofinal system of open coverings
3427	  % of $U$.
3428	  % \item For every $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$ we have
3429	  % $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$.
3430	  % \end{enumerate}
3431	  % Then $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$ and any $U \in \mathcal{B}$."
3432	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3433	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.}
3434	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let
3435	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and suppose there is a set
3436	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of open coverings such that: (1) every \(\mathcal{U} :
3437	  U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) has \(U, U_i \in \mathcal{B}\) and
3438	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\); (2) for each
3439	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
3440	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
3441	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,
3442	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Then
3443	  \[
3444	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0 \text{ and any }
3445	      U \in \mathcal{B}.
3446	  \]
3447	  This is the basis-comparison vehicle that upgrades the standard-cover {\v C}ech
3448	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} to the Serre vanishing of
3449	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}: instantiating \(\mathcal{B}\) as the affine
3450	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly
3451	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. The formal target of this lemma is the
3452	  \emph{vanishing} conclusion \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\) under
3453	  hypotheses (1)--(3), not an explicit isomorphism \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
3454	  \mathcal{F}) \cong H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of {\v C}ech and sheaf cohomology; only
3455	  the positive-degree vanishing is needed downstream, and it is all the statement
3456	  establishes.
3457	\end{lemma}

3543	  \(H^0(U, -) = \Gamma(U, -)\) and the section-level surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) \to
3544	  \mathcal{Q}(U)\), yields the base case \(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
3545	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}). Feeding \(\mathcal{Q}\) back as
3546	  \(\mathcal{F}\) and iterating the dimension shift — legitimate because \(\mathcal{Q}\)
3547	  is again a module with vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\) —
3548	  gives \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and every \(U \in \mathcal{B}\)
3549	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}), which is the assertion.
3550	
3551	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
3552	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is
3553	  condition (3), which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover
3554	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
3555	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the
3556	  graph is acyclic.
3557	\end{proof}

3559	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
3560	
3561	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
3562	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
3563	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
3564	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
3565	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
3566	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
3567	
3568	\begin{lemma}
3569	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
3570	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
3571	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
3572	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
3573	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3574	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
3575	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
3576	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
3577	  % \begin{equation}
3578	  % \label{equation-extended}
3579	  % 0 \to
3580	  % M \to
3581	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
3582	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
3583	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
3584	  % \ldots
3585	  % \end{equation}
3586	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
3587	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
3588	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
3589	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
3590	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
3591	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3592	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
3593	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
3594	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
3595	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
3596	  \[
3597	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
3598	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
3599	    \qquad
3600	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
3601	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
3602	  \]
3603	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
3604	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
3605	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
3606	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
3607	\end{lemma}
3608	
3609	\begin{proof}
3610	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
3611	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
3612	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
3613	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
3614	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
3615	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
3616	  localisations
3617	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
3618	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
3619	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
3620	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
3621	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
3622	\end{proof}

3624	\begin{lemma}
3625	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
3626	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3627	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
3628	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
3629	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
3630	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
3631	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
3632	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
3633	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
3634	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
3635	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
3636	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
3637	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
3638	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
3639	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
3640	  \[
3641	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
3642	      \;\cong\;
3643	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
3644	  \]
3645	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
3646	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
3647	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
3648	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
3649	\end{lemma}
3650	
3651	\begin{proof}
3652	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
3653	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
3654	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
3655	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
3656	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
3657	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
3658	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
3659	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
3660	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
3661	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
3662	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
3663	  additive homology-preserving functor.
3664	\end{proof}

3666	\begin{lemma}
3667	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
3668	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
3669	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
3670	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
3671	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3672	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
3673	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
3674	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
3675	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
3676	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
3677	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3678	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
3679	  % associated to the presheaves
3680	  % $$
3681	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
3682	  % $$
3683	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
3684	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
3685	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
3686	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
3687	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
3688	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
3689	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
3690	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
3691	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3692	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
3693	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
3694	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
3695	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
3696	  \[
3697	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3698	      \;\cong\;
3699	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3700	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3701	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
3702	  \]
3703	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
3704	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
3705	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
3706	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
3707	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
3708	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
3709	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
3710	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
3711	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
3712	
3713	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
3714	  of the preimage,
3715	  \[
3716	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
3717	      \;=\;
3718	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3719	  \]
3720	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
3721	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
3722	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
3723	\end{lemma}
3724	
3725	\begin{proof}
3726	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3727	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3728	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
3729	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
3730	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
3731	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
3732	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
3733	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
3734	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
3735	  \[
3736	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3737	      \;\cong\;
3738	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3739	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3740	  \]
3741	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
3742	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
3743	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
3744	\end{proof}

3746	\begin{lemma}
3747	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
3748	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3749	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
3750	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
3751	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3752	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
3753	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
3754	  % L180--199).
3755	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3756	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3757	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
3758	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3759	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
3760	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
3761	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
3762	  \to S\). Then:
3763	  \begin{enumerate}
3764	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
3765	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
3766	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
3767	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
3768	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
3769	      \(k \geq 0\),
3770	      \[
3771	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
3772	      \]
3773	  \end{enumerate}
3774	\end{lemma}
3775	
3776	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
3777	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
3778	% the sheaf
3779	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3780	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
3781	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
3782	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
3783	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
3784	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
3785	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
3786	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
3787	\begin{proof}
3788	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
3789	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
3790	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
3791	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
3792	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
3793	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
3794	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
3795	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
3796	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
3797	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
3798	
3799	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3800	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
3801	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
3802	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
3803	
3804	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
3805	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
3806	  sheafification of
3807	  \[
3808	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
3809	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3810	  \]
3811	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
3812	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
3813	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
3814	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
3815	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
3816	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
3817	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
3818	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3819	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
3820	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
3821	
3822	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
3823	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
3824	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
3825	  for every \(k\),
3826	  \[
3827	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
3828	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
3829	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
3830	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
3831	  \]
3832	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
3833	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
3834	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
3835	\end{proof}

3837	\begin{lemma}
3838	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
3839	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
3840	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
3841	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3842	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3843	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
3844	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
3845	  % L180--199).
3846	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3847	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3848	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
3849	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3850	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative affine vanishing).}
3851	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
3852	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\mathfrak{U}\) a finite affine open
3853	  cover of \(X\) with all intersections affine. Then for every \(p \geq 0\) the
3854	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =
3855	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\)
3856	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
3857	  \[
3858	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
3859	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.
3860	  \]
3861	\end{lemma}
3862	
3863	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
3864	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
3865	% the sheaf
3866	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3867	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
3868	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
3869	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
3870	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
3871	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
3872	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
3873	% opens we win."
3874	\begin{proof}
3875	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3876	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
3877	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
3878	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
3879	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
3880	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3881	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
3882	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
3883	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
3884	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
3885	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
3886	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
3887	
3888	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
3889	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
3890	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
3891	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
3892	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
3893	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
3894	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
3895	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
3896	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative

**Naturality correctness.** `lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural` states the commuting square:
```
  Γ(U, I) ——g_U——> Γ(U, Q)
     ≅|                 |≅
  H⁰(U,I) ——H⁰(g)——> H⁰(U,Q)
```
where both verticals are the H⁰≅Γ equivalence. The proof section explicitly states: "In particular, when g_U is surjective so is H⁰(U,g) — the transfer used by the surjectivity step of Lemma lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing." This is precisely the content consumed: `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing` applies the covariant Ext LES at `n=1`, obtains `δ: H¹(U,F)→H¹(U,I)`, and must kill it via `H⁰(U,I)→H⁰(U,Q)` surjective. That surjectivity is exactly the naturality transfer of `I(U)↠Q(U)` (from `lem:ses_cech_h1`) across the H⁰≅Γ iso. The connection is correct and precisely scoped.

**`\uses` check for wrappers.** `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing` carries `\uses{..., lem:absolute_cohomology_zero, lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural}` — both iso and naturality cited. DAG edge is live (`unknown_uses: []`). Correct.

| `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` | `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` | Top 01EO: assembles four sub-lemmas under conditions (1)(2)(3) | ✓ | n/a |

The quotient `Q = coker(I(U)→F(U))` is built as a sheaf of modules (injective quotient in `X.Modules`); it is not quasi-coherent in general. The blueprint does not impose quasi-coherence on Q at any step. The abstraction is correct.
